options.name: --license-file-path
options.description:
If specified, a registered version of ArmDot reads a license key from the
file <license file path>.

options.name: --arguments-file
options.description:
If specified, ArmDotConsole reads arguments from the file path <path>.
Use this option when it's impossible to pass parameters in the usual way
because the command line length reaches its maximum.

options.name: --input-assembly
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole obfuscates the assembly <assembly path>.

options.name: --key-file
options.description: Specifies the path to the file containing the key pair, which is used to

options.name: --enable-obfuscate-names
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole obfuscates names of the assembly specified by

options.name: --protect-embedded-resources
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole protects embedded resources of the assembly

options.name: --enable-virtualize-code
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole virtualizes code of the assembly specified

options.name: --enable-hide-strings
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole protects strings used by the assembly specified

options.name: --build
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole builds a project.

options.name: --project-path
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole uses this project either to build it or

options.name: --generate-license-key
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole generates a license key

options.name: --generate-hardware-id
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole generates a hardware id

options.name: --license-user-name
options.description: If specified, ArmDotConsole uses this user name to generate

options.name: --hardware
options.description: Comma separated list of hardware that is used to generate a hardware id.
